title: AI forex trading bot
author: Pale-Cap1820
selftext:
Everyone, I just wanted to make a post talking about my experience with OpenClaw and trying to automate trading. I figured I’d throw this out there in case anyone is interested in hearing how it’s been going for me so far.

I’ve always wanted to be a day trader, but I have the same problem most people do. I’ve got emotions, and I don’t have all day to sit there staring at charts and analyzing everything.

So when OpenClaw started getting popular, I thought, why not see if I can build a group of sub-agents to trade meme coins for me on paper. Surprisingly, it actually worked pretty well at first. For a little while I was seeing overnight gains of over 100%.

The problem is, that only lasted about four days before everything started getting buggy. My agents started messing up my Discord displays, things got unstable, and the costs started climbing way faster than I expected. So I decided to fully reset everything and try again, this time focusing more on sniping earlier launches instead of trading higher market cap coins.

That setup did alright too, but honestly the sniper bot was pretty bad. At the same time, I’m self-taught with all of this. I’ve basically been learning as I go, using ChatGPT and OpenClaw side by side to build things, fix things, and figure things out.

After a few more days, it started turning into the same story again. More bugs, more problems, and more money getting burned just trying to keep it all running. I was spending around $20 a day just trying to manage everything and keep costs under control.

One big thing I’ve learned from all this is that if you’re getting started, have a real plan before you start throwing a bunch of ideas at the wall. Constantly creating new things and rebuilding stuff gets expensive way faster than just building one solid thing and letting it run.

So after that, I wiped everything again and decided to move away from crypto and focus more on day trading. It felt like a better space to work in because it’s less chaotic, less volatile, and a little more structured, which makes it easier for algorithms to actually have a fair shot.

Since then, I’ve built a strategy I’m running now that’s sitting around a 68 to 70 percent win rate, with about 50 to 60 trades a month and an average return around 1.7 to 1.8R. I also figured out what I needed to do to connect it to a funded account, and now I’m just debugging it day by day.

Yesterday it placed its first ever paper trade on a practice funded account, which was honestly a huge milestone for me.

Today was a little rough because I spent about two hours debugging it during the Japan open, so of course I missed the actual trades while I was trying to fix everything. But that’s part of it. We’re still making progress.

I mainly wanted to share this to document the process and maybe inspire some of you that are building stuff too. It definitely hasn’t been smooth, but I’ve learned a lot, and it’s been cool seeing things slowly start to come together.

Thanks for reading, and if people are interested, I can keep posting updates as I go.
